verdict: Gate battery run live at HEAD 9cb5e7a (RTX 5090, RAM cage, 5 GPU boots ~15 min total). TWO findings: (A) the lossless-APC canonical-publish seam (W1+W2) is STILL LIVE-INERT -- publish fired 0x on matched-20, never-train, AND the gt4 2048-crosser; it changes zero serving bits; the 3 named wiring gaps stand. (B) SEPARATELY, the post-Stage-3 engine is ALREADY effectively cache-lossless on the QUALITY axis: exact_args is byte-stable and fully APC-invariant (matched-20 47/63, never-train 83/184, identical warm==cold==gate-on==gate-off), and cache-on self-consistency is 174/184 (matched-20 62/63) with every residual a quality-neutral fp-epsilon near-tie. The certificate the battery targeted (cache-on quality == fresh) is effectively already TRUE via Stage-3, NOT via the seam. The remaining gap is strict BITWISE losslessness on near-tie tokens, which the inert seam targets but (per rootcause Refinement 1) a chunked kernel cannot fully close anyway -- needs the sequential-recurrent republish design.
promotion: canonical-publish seam NOT promoted; default stays OFF; not pushed. Stage-3 shipped path stands and is the current cache-lossless-on-quality engine.
